[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] * [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}}[[Category:11]] {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} |shrine_rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == 境内 == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:to be resorted]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}}

At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:to be resorted]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}}

The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:to be resorted]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} [[Category:New]]

[[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] * [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}}[[Category:11]] {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} |shrine_rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:to be resorted]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} [[Category:New]]

Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|12=draft}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}}

* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市|12=draft}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=draft}}

Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|12=draft}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|12=draft}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}}

* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} |shrine_rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|12=draft|12=draft}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}}

revision.text:
{{otheruses|新居浜市角野新田町にある[[神社]]|新居浜市磯浦町の大山祇神社|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} {{神社 |名称 = 大山積神社 |画像 = [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |所在地 = 愛媛県新居浜市角野新田町3丁目13−13 |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]]
[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[1691年]] |本殿 = |別名 = 山根大山積神社
山神さん（さんじんさん） |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''大山積神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]角野新田町にある[[神社]]。[[住友グループ]]の守護神で、[[新居浜市]]や[[住友グループ]]の礎を作った[[別子銅山]]の守護神が奉られている。 境内には[[別子銅山記念館]]が整備されており、[[山根公園]]にも隣接している。境内から新居浜市山根市民グラウンドを臨む、新居浜市街を一望できる位置に鎮座する。 ==祭神== * [[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（[[古事記]]では大山積神の孫にあたる。[[稲荷神社|稲荷神]]） 神社系列としては、[[三島・大山祇信仰]]と[[稲荷神|稲荷信仰]]の系列に属する。 ==歴史== 大山積神社の歴史は、[[1691年]]の[[別子銅山]]開坑に始まる。同時期に[[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]より勧請・創建された。全国の[[鉱山]]に勧請された鉱山型大山祇神社（[[三島・大山祇信仰]]）の中でも古い社の一つであり、大三島の大山祇神社発行の資料によると、[[1605年]]の[[佐渡金山]]の[[大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|大山祇神社]]に次いで創建が早い[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)]（大山祇神社社務所）。鉱夫が入抗する際には、必ず坑口に奉られる御祭神を拝礼したという。 当初は[[別子山村]]足谷の縁起の端に位置していたが、[[1893年]]に足谷の目出度町に遷座。[[1927年]]には新居浜市内に移転し、[[内宮神社]]境内に仮遷座のち、[[1928年]]に現在地にと、採掘が海側に移動するにつれ遷座していった。現在地に移った翌年には、磯浦町にあった住友グループ各事業の守護神も移された。 [[1973年]]に別子銅山は閉山するが、現在も住友グループ各社により祭祀が行われている。新年祭や例大祭は新居浜在所のグループ各社の参列が習わしである現地案内板。所有・管理は[[別子銅山]]を経営し、現在でも多数の鉱山を操業する[[住友金属鉱山]]が行っており、本社屋上や各事業所にも大山積神社が勧請されている[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ フォトギャラリー（住友金属鉱山）]。 1975年、住友グループ21社により境内に[[別子銅山記念館]]が整備された。 == 境内 == * 本殿拝殿 * 大鉑 - 拝殿の向って右にある。毎年奉納されていた巨大な銅鉱石。展示されているのは1973年に奉納された最後のもの。 * 奥の宮社 - 本殿から100mほど上がって東に100mほどで石鳥居の先にあり、背後から新居浜市を一望できる。生子山（煙突山）の頂上にあたる。 Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|拝殿 Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|本殿 Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|大鉑 Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|奥の宮社 == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|銅精錬所の煙突]] * [[別子銅山記念館]] - 境内にある博物館 * 蒸気機関車・かご電車 - ドイツ・クラウス社製で、初代[[坊ちゃん列車]]と同型のものである。鉱山鉄道用に実際に使用されていた。 * 煙突山の煙突（旧山根製錬所煙突） - 本殿から100mほど上がって西に100mほど。国の登録有形文化財 == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:産業遺産]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:別子銅山]] [[Category:住友金属鉱山]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] to [[Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]

A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]

* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|ja|愛媛県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niihama, Ehime|ja|新居浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ukekekekekke Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]

According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]]

Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:住友金属鉱山]]

A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:住友金属鉱山]] [[Category:別子銅山]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:産業遺産]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Sumitomo Metal Mining
revision.text:
* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Niihama|Niihama, Ehime]], Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:別子銅山]] [[Category:新居浜市の歴史]] [[Category:産業遺産]]
