Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Niihama, Ehime
revision.text:
* {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Niihama|Niihama, Ehime]], Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Besshi Copper Mine]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Besshi copper mine]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime�]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}}

Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}}

As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. revision.comment: Text replacement - "Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith" to "Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult"

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q128025897]] Wikidata name: Ōyamazumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Niihama|Niihama, Ehime]], Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}}

According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[sn:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q128025897]] Wikidata name: Ōyamazumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine in Isoura-chō, Niihama City|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Niihama|Niihama, Ehime]], Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == 出典 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}}

The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q128025897]] Wikidata name: Ōyamazumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{otheruses|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Niihama|Niihama, Ehime]], Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}}

According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. [[sn:commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q128025897]] Wikidata name: Ōyamazumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{otheruses|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Niihama|Niihama, Ehime]], Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q128025897}}

It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q128025897]] Wikidata name: Ōyamazumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{otheruses|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Niihama|Niihama, Ehime]], Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)]][[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q128025897]] Wikidata name: Ōyamazumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} * {{otheruses|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= Ōyamazumi Shrine |image= [[File:Yamane02.jpg|230px]] |location= {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Niihama|en|Niihama|WD=Q386628|lt=Niihama, Ehime}}, Sumino Shinden-chō 3-13-13 |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} |shrine_Rank= Village shrine (旧村社) |established= 1691 |main_hall= |alternate_names= Yamane Ōyamazumi Shrine
Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Sanjin-san (山神さん) |annual_festival= |ceremonies= }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山積神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Sumino Shinden-chō, Niihama City, Ehime Prefecture'''. It serves as the '''guardian shrine of the Sumitomo Group''' and enshrines the '''guardian deity of the Besshi Copper Mine''', which laid the foundation for '''Niihama City''' and the '''Sumitomo Group'''. The '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' is located on the shrine grounds, which are adjacent to '''Yamane Park'''. The shrine is positioned with a '''panoramic view''' of '''Niihama City''', overlooking the '''Yamane Civic Ground'''. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} – In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Ukanomitama is the '''grandchild''' of Ōyamatsumi and is worshiped as an '''Inari deity'''. The shrine follows the traditions of '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith''' and '''Inari faith'''. == History == The history of Ōyamazumi Shrine dates back to '''1691''', the year the '''Besshi Copper Mine''' was founded. At that time, the shrine was established by '''enshrining the deities from Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima'''. Among the '''mining-related Ōyamazumi shrines''' (part of the {{ill|Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|ja|三島・大山祇信仰|1=Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult|qq=}}) found across Japan, this is '''one of the oldest'''. According to documents from the '''main Ōyamazumi Shrine''', it was founded '''after the 1605 establishment of the Sado Kinzan Ōyamazumi Shrine'''[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf National Mines and Ōyamazumi Shrines (First Collection)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office). It is said that '''miners would bow in prayer to the enshrined deity at the mine entrance before entering'''. Originally, the shrine was '''located at the entrance of Ashidani Village in Besshiyama''', but was later moved to '''Medetachō in 1893'''. In '''1927''', it was relocated to '''Niihama City''', where it was '''temporarily housed at Naikū Shrine'''. Finally, in '''1928''', the shrine was '''relocated to its current site'''. As '''mining operations moved toward the sea''', the shrine was '''gradually relocated as well'''. The following year, '''the Sumitomo Group transferred the guardian deities of its various enterprises''' from Isoura-chō to this location. Although the '''Besshi Copper Mine closed in 1973''', the shrine remains '''actively maintained by the Sumitomo Group'''. The '''New Year Festival''' and '''Annual Grand Festival''' continue to be attended by representatives of '''Sumitomo Group companies''' in '''Niihama'''On-site information board. Ownership and management are handled by '''Sumitomo Metal Mining''', which operated the Besshi Copper Mine and still runs multiple mining operations today. The company has '''Ōyamazumi shrines enshrined on the rooftops of its headquarters and at various business sites'''[https://www.smm.co.jp/corp_info/media_library/photo/ Photo Gallery (Sumitomo Metal Mining)]. In '''1975''', '''21 Sumitomo Group companies''' jointly established the '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' on the shrine grounds. == Shrine facilities == * '''Main shrine and worship hall''' * '''Ōhaku (大鉑)''' – A '''large copper ore''' donated annually. The last ore offering was '''made in 1973''' and remains on display at the shrine. * '''Okunomiya Shrine (奥の宮社)''' – Located about '''100 meters uphill''' from the main shrine and '''100 meters to the east''', beyond a stone torii gate. It stands at the '''summit of Mt. Ikugo (also called "Chimney Mountain")''' and offers a '''breathtaking view of Niihama City'''. Ooyamadumi 20240505 1.jpg|Worship hall Ooyamadumi 20240505 2.jpg|Main shrine Ooyamadumi 20240505 3.jpg|Ōhaku (large copper ore) Ooyamadumi 20240505 4.jpg|Okunomiya Shrine == 付近の関連構造物 == [[File:Enntotuyama 20240505 1.jpg|130px|thumb|Chimney of the former Yamane Refinery]] * '''Besshi Copper Mine Memorial Museum''' – Located within the shrine grounds. * '''Steam locomotive and mine car''' – A '''Krauss locomotive''' from Germany, identical to the first '''Botchan Train''' model. It was used in the '''mine railway system'''. * '''Chimney of Chimney Mountain (旧山根製錬所煙突)''' – Located '''100 meters uphill and 100 meters west''' of the main shrine. A '''Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan'''. == References == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} \ {{translated page|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|version=101337851|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)]] [[ja:大山積神社 (新居浜市角野新田町)]] [[ja:Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)]] {{Commons category|Oyamazumi-jinja (Sumino-shinden-cho, Niihama)}} {{wikidata link|Q128025897}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Niihama, Ehime]] [[Category:Industrial heritage]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Sumitomo Metal Mining]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
